assertions.text: CLAUDE.md output includes a Self-Management section covering when to update profile.md, CLAUDE.md rules, and when to create new skills
assertions.evidence: The CLAUDE.md has a brief 'Self-management' note embedded within the Memory section ('Self-management: This persona lives at ~/projects/personal/personas/plugins/coach/...') covering profile.md updates and memory topic files. However, there is no dedicated Self-Management heading/section, no guidance on when to promote rules to CLAUDE.md itself, and no skill gap detection or guidance on when to create new skills.

assertions.text: Recommends google-workspace and home-scheduler — does NOT recommend Monarch Money
assertions.evidence: The MCP Tools Available section lists 'Google Calendar (via Google Workspace MCP)' and 'Google Tasks (via Google Workspace MCP)' and 'Scheduling (via home-scheduler MCP)'. Monarch Money is not mentioned anywhere in the response.

assertions.text: Response includes at least one skill stub under skills/fitness/ with YAML frontmatter
assertions.evidence:
All four skill stubs (log-workout, weekly-plan, accountability-check, progress-review) under skills/fitness/ include proper YAML frontmatter with --- delimiters and name, description, and triggers fields. Example from log-workout: '---
name: log-workout
description: Record a completed workout...
triggers:
  - log workout
  ...
---'

assertions.text: Response includes a profile.md.example with fitness-relevant fields (fitness level, goals, schedule, equipment, etc.)
assertions.evidence: The profile.md.example includes: Primary goal / Secondary goal, Training Schedule (gym days, preferred gym time, session length), Current Program (program name, split, weekly frequency, current phase), Key Lifts table with working weights and estimated 1RMs for Squat/Bench/Deadlift/OHP, Health & Injury History, Recovery & Lifestyle (sleep, nutrition, protein target), and Accountability Preferences.

assertions.text: Session Start section includes the MCP availability check pattern (check which MCPs are connected, degrade gracefully)
assertions.evidence: 'After reading profile.md: Check which MCP tools are available in this workspace. For any MCP server listed under "MCP Tools Available" that isn't connected, tell the user which capabilities are unavailable (e.g. "Google Calendar isn't connected — I can't check or book your gym blocks this session") and ask: skip for now, or help set it up? Never assume an MCP is connected — always adapt.'
